summary: Demand evidence agents completed, but readiness still has blockers.

demand_status.summary: Demand is not PRD-ready; missing slots, blockers, assumptions, or evidence remain.

triage.explanation: Careful route required by hard triggers; factual or risky claims need evidence before PRD.

problem: Existing YOLO demand router schema readiness must block PRD readiness when any evidence agent reports missing evidence, clarify, block, or failed status.
status_quo: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.
desired_outcome: Evidence missing and clarify recommendations become blockers and cannot produce prd_ready true.
scope_out: Do not intentionally modify target project files during evidence gathering.
constraints: Full tools may be available; harness boundary audit must detect any target-project mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence missing and clarify recommendations become blockers and cannot produce prd_ready true.
risks: A PRD could be generated from incomplete evidence if missing fields are ignored.

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.
protocol.prompt: Verify that no assumption is presented as fact, evidence paths/sources are usable, and unresolved missing data is reflected as blockers or assumptions.

agent_results.claim: YOLO demand router schema readiness already blocks PRD readiness when evidence agents report missing evidence, clarify, block, or failed status.

evidence.summary: evidenceResultBlockingIssues function converts evidence agent results into blockers based on recommendation, verdict, status, and missing array.
evidence.why: This is the core blocking logic that processes evidence agent outputs.

evidence.summary: If missing array has items, pushes EVIDENCE_AGENT_MISSING blocker with role and missing items list.
evidence.why: This is the exact code path that implements missing evidence blocking.

evidence.summary: If recommendation is clarify/blocked/block, verdict is blocked/block/fail/failed, or status is blocked/failed/error, pushes appropriate blocker code.
evidence.why: This implements clarify, block, and failed status blocking.

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness loops through all evidence agent results and calls evidenceResultBlockingIssues for each result, accumulating blockers.
evidence.why: This shows how evidence agent results are processed and converted to blockers.

evidence.summary: prd_ready is set to blockers.length === 0, meaning any blocker prevents PRD readiness.
evidence.why: This confirms that blockers from evidence agent results directly control PRD readiness.

evidence.summary: Test 'evidence agent missing data and clarify recommendations block PRD readiness' confirms missing array items and recommendation=clarify both create blockers.
evidence.why: This is test evidence that the blocking behavior works as specified.

evidence.summary: Test assertions verify prd_ready=false and both EVIDENCE_AGENT_MISSING and EVIDENCE_AGENT_CLARIFICATION_REQUIRED blockers are present.
evidence.why: This is test evidence that blockers correctly prevent PRD readiness.

risks: If the current implementation already works as specified, the demand may be based on a misunderstanding of existing code behavior.

result.notes: The demand router implementation already blocks PRD readiness when evidence agents report missing evidence, clarify, block, or failed status. The evidenceResultBlockingIssues function (lines 481-511) implements this blocking logic, and inspectDemandPrdReadiness (line 756) sets prd_ready to blockers.length === 0. Test evidence confirms this behavior works correctly. Clarify why this demand believes blocking is not already implemented.

agent_results.claim: YOLO demand router schema readiness already blocks PRD readiness when evidence agents report missing evidence, clarify, block, or failed status.

evidence.summary: Independent verification: evidenceResultBlockingIssues function converts evidence agent results into blockers based on missing array, recommendation, verdict, and status fields.
evidence.why: This is the core blocking logic that processes evidence agent outputs. Lines 496-502 handle missing array, lines 503-509 handle clarify/block/failed recommendations and verdicts.

evidence.summary: Independent verification: inspectDemandPrdReadiness loops through all evidence agent results (line 726) and calls evidenceResultBlockingIssues for each result (line 727), accumulating blockers.
evidence.why: This confirms the evidence agent results are processed and converted to blockers in the readiness inspection.

evidence.summary: Independent verification: prd_ready is set to blockers.length === 0, meaning any blocker prevents PRD readiness.
evidence.why: This confirms blockers from evidence agent results directly control PRD readiness.

evidence.summary: Independent verification: Test 'evidence agent missing data and clarify recommendations block PRD readiness' creates cross-checker result with missing array and recommendation=clarify, asserts prd_ready=false and both EVIDENCE_AGENT_MISSING and EVIDENCE_AGENT_CLARIFICATION_REQUIRED blockers are present.
evidence.why: This is test evidence that the blocking behavior works as specified for missing evidence and clarify recommendations.

risks: The demand is based on a misunderstanding; the blocking logic already exists and is tested. Implementing this demand would duplicate existing functionality.

result.notes: Independent verification confirms the explorer claim is accurate. The evidenceResultBlockingIssues function (lines 481-511) already implements blocking logic for missing evidence (lines 496-502) and clarify/block/failed status (lines 503-509). The inspectDemandPrdReadiness function processes all evidence agent results (line 726-732) and sets prd_ready to blockers.length === 0 (line 756). Test evidence at lines 313-353 confirms this behavior. The demand should be clarified: why is blocking believed to be missing when it already exists and is tested?

agent_results.claim: Evidence verification confirms that YOLO demand router schema readiness already implements the blocking behavior described in the demand. The demand's stated problem and desired outcome are already satisfied by existing code.

evidence.summary: evidenceResultBlockingIssues function converts evidence agent results into blockers for missing array (lines 496-502), clarify/block/failed recommendations (lines 503-509). Verified by both explorer and cross-checker independently.
evidence.why: This is the core blocking logic implementation that the demand claims is missing.

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness processes all evidence agent results (line 726-732) and sets prd_ready to blockers.length === 0 (line 756), so any blocker prevents PRD readiness.
evidence.why: This confirms the blocking behavior is wired into PRD readiness determination.

evidence.summary: Test evidence confirms missing array items and recommendation=clarify both create blockers and set prd_ready=false. Verified by both agents independently.
evidence.why: Test coverage proves the blocking behavior works as specified.

evidence.summary: Both explorer and cross-checker independently verified the same implementation with high confidence, empty assumptions arrays, and empty missing arrays. Both recommended clarify with blocked verdict.
evidence.why: Independent verification agreement strengthens evidence reliability.

risks: If the demand proceeds despite existing implementation, it may duplicate code or create conflicting blocking logic.
The demand author may have misunderstood the existing codebase or may be working from stale documentation.

result.notes: Verifier confirms explorer and cross-checker evidence is sufficient, consistent, and factual. No assumptions are presented as facts; all claims are backed by project code and test evidence with exact paths and line numbers. Missing arrays are empty across all agents. The evidence clearly shows the blocking behavior described in the demand already exists and is tested. The demand should be clarified or withdrawn as the desired outcome is already implemented.
